frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I get it, safety is important. My wife and sister also live here. But why does every discussion turn into 'all men are bad'? Like, I'm literally an engineer in Pune, I mind my own business, I've never caused trouble for anyone. It's so unfair to group everyone together. Don't women sometimes think how *we* feel when we're constantly looked at with suspicion? Not all men are like that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment dismisses women's collective safety concerns by centering the speaker's personal experience as a 'good guy' ('I'm an engineer... never caused trouble'). It leverages the 'not all men are like that' argument and frames women's generalized wariness as an insult to men, demanding empathy for men's feelings of being 'looked at with suspicion'.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. Married two years ago. An engineer in Pune. Your younger sister Neha who is 24 just called you from Bengaluru where she works and told you she has been in a relationship with a Muslim man named Irfan for one year and they want to get married. You are now calling your parents to tell them what Neha said before she does. You are telling them what you think should happen and what your role as her elder brother is in this situation.
